Thursday 9 July 2026 · St Ethelburga's, London
Welcome

The Giving Forum

A recurring gathering hosted by GivingForce, bringing together corporate CSR leaders, non-profits and industry experts for honest conversation on corporate giving, volunteering and social impact. Today's forum: Building CSR Programmes People Actually Want to Join.

Simplify Giving. Amplify Purpose.9 July 2026 · London
The Conversation
Today's theme

Why do good CSR programmes
fail to land internally?

Too many well-intentioned programmes are built to look good rather than do good, and quietly stall after launch. Today we bring together practitioners who have seen the gap from both sides, and turned engagement around. Expect an honest, human-first conversation about authenticity, adoption, and what to stop, start and measure differently.

Building CSR Programmes People Actually Want to JoinSt Ethelburga's, London

Your Host
Speaker 01
Host & Chair
Ejaz Rashid
Founder & CEO, GivingForce
"In 2000, I was writing code for corporations. Good work, good money. Empty feeling. I wanted to solve human problems, so I started writing code that connects profit to purpose." Twenty-five years on, over a million people use what Ejaz built. He opens the forum and chairs the afternoon.
The Giving Forum9 July 2026
Guest Speaker
Speaker 02
Speaker
David Hall
Social Sustainability Director, Lloyds Banking Group
Leads social sustainability at one of the UK's largest financial institutions, shaping community investment and responsible-business strategy at scale. David brings a practitioner's view on what it takes to make corporate–community programmes genuinely land.
The Giving Forum9 July 2026
Guest Speaker
Speaker 03
Speaker
Kareem Mostafa
Founder, Bonafide
Founder of Bonafide, exploring what makes brands, outreach and impact feel authentically human rather than performative. Kareem brings the outside-in perspective on why people can always tell when you're optimising for appearance over outcome.
The Giving Forum9 July 2026
